title: Approved-source list + license-verification rules (PD/CC only)
context: Every card in the open-flashcards ecosystem must originate from openly-licensed source material. Without a curated approved-source list and rigorous license-verification rules, contributors risk unknowingly using copyrighted content (textbooks, proprietary question banks, copyrighted images), exposing the project to legal risk and undermining its integrity. The 'unknown = excluded' rule must be defined and operationalized before any deck authoring begins.
objective: Produce an approved-source list covering PD, CC0, CC-BY, CC-BY-SA, OER, and official open syllabi, plus the license-verification rules that all deck tasks and the automated license gate will enforce.
acceptanceCriteria: Produces an approved-source list (PD, CC0, CC-BY, CC-BY-SA, OER, official open syllabi) with a per-source license note and SPDX identifier.
Defines the 'unknown = excluded' rule and categorical exclusion of existing third-party decks as sources.
Specifies how share-alike (CC-BY-SA) and non-commercial (NC) terms constrain the deck's chosen output license.
Specifies separate media (image/audio) license verification with its own attribution requirements.
Document is versioned, published, and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
output: A versioned approved-source list and license-verification rules document (docs/approved-sources-and-license-rules.md), CC-BY-4.0, covering text and media sources, SPDX identifiers, share-alike/NC constraints, and the 'unknown = excluded' rule.
